title: Home MCP Compliance Lab — Audit Event Schema
description: Platform-level audit event schema for MCP tool invocations and runtime secret retrieval. Version 0 foundation. All events emitted by platform-managed services must conform to this structure. Secret values must never appear in any field.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema. Increment on breaking changes.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event instance. Recommended: UUID v4.

event_type.description: Categorizes the event. See defined event types below.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the event occurred.

platform.description: Identifier for the platform emitting the event. Always 'home-mcp-lab' for this system.

project_id.description: Identifier of the project whose MCP server emitted this event.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the agent or Claude Code session that triggered the action.

mcp_server.description: Identifier of the MCP server that processed the tool call or performed the retrieval.

action.description: The specific action taken. For tool.invocation: the tool name. For secret.retrieval: the retrieval mechanism identifier.

correlation_id.description: Identifier linking related events within a single workflow or agent session.

metadata.description: Flexible extension field for event-type-specific context. Must never contain secret values, credentials, tokens, or sensitive data. See event type definitions for expected metadata fields.

event_type.tool.invocation.description: Emitted when an MCP server executes a tool call on behalf of an agent.

metadata_fields.tool_name: string — name of the tool invoked (same as action field)
metadata_fields.arguments_summary: string — non-sensitive summary or schema of arguments; must not include secret or PII values
metadata_fields.execution_duration_ms: number — duration of tool execution in milliseconds
metadata_fields.result_summary: string — non-sensitive description of the outcome; must not include raw result data if sensitive

event_type.secret.retrieval.description: Emitted when a service or agent attempts to retrieve a secret via the platform-approved mechanism. Secret values must never appear in this event.

metadata_fields.secret_identifier: string — non-sensitive reference to which secret was requested (e.g., a vault path, record title, or identifier); must not be the secret value
metadata_fields.environment_context: string — 'service' or 'cli' — describes the execution context
metadata_fields.retrieval_mechanism: string — identifier of the tool or method used (e.g., 'keeper-commander')
metadata_fields.failure_reason: string — present only on failure; non-sensitive description of why retrieval failed

constraints: Secret values must never appear in any event field, including metadata.
Metadata must contain only non-sensitive, non-PII data.
All events must include the full set of required top-level fields.
The schema_version field must be updated when breaking changes are introduced.
This schema is platform-owned. Projects must not define their own audit event schemas.

metadata.failure_reason: Session token not present in service execution context
